page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration provides 1.0 thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is not active on this instance. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.r7t.8xlarge mostly in the average tier, with a few strong performance highlights. The instance achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1842.78 and a multi-core score of 31113.04. Memory bandwidth tests show 1708.58 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 153.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 68.8 GB/sec for large-block writes. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs in the top 25% tier at 163.36 GB/sec, and text generation for a small 135M LLM model also ranks in the top 25% at 312.77 tokens/sec. Other metrics, such as PassMark CPU (36647.59) and database operations (14663.56), remain in the middle 50% range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7t.8xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Given its hardware profile and benchmark performance, this instance is suited for database operations, memory-cached web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale text processing or LLM inference tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7t.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate solid middle-tier capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 36647.59 and a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 163.36 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong text generation performance for small language models. The ecs.r7t.8xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching layers, and software compilation.
